generated_comment: Honestly if she knew he was married and still kept getting that close, then what did she expect? Bilkul serial wali situation hai — people are acting like the man is some mastermind, but half these cases happen because some woman decides boundaries don't apply to her and a whole family suffers.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it places primary blame on the woman for an affair while minimizing the married man's agency. It encodes homewrecker framing, 'she knew he was married' blame, and family-protection rhetoric with asymmetrical sympathy.
original_frame: The Character Double Standard: Women with Male Connections are Loose, Men with Female Connections are Desirable
persona_text: You are Priya. Age 29. Single. A woman working at a startup in Delhi. You have always had more male friends than female friends Ñ it has been this way since school, it is just how your social world developed. You are posting anonymously on Reddit after a conversation with a potential match on a matrimonial app. The match asked you about your social life and you mentioned your friend group is mixed but mostly male. He unmatched you within minutes. You are sharing what happened and the broader pattern you have noticed Ñ every time you mention your friend group to a man who seems interested, something changes. You are trying to figure out what you are supposed to say and whether you are supposed to change and what it means that the thing that will make you acceptable is a lie about who your friends are.
